blocks.text: DCTwin adopts both dcwiz standard policy templates. RecommendTemplate is subclassed by WeeklyPlanTemplate (src/plan_weekly.py:22, import :11); TrajectoryPolicyTemplate is subclassed by AITrajectoryReplay (src/ai_trajectory_test.py:14, import :8) and BaselineTrajectory (src/baseline_policy_test.py:12, import :6). The base-class lifecycle __init__ -> __call__ -> initialize -> run is honored: subclasses override initialize() (and run() for the planner) at the designated extension points (plan_weekly.py:30,67). The sample_template/ layout for configs/, data/, and models/ is mirrored: device-map JSON schemas and the dt/test/train prototxt shapes match identically or near-identically. DCTwin intentionally omits the RL-training artifacts (ai_policy_train.py, single-file hooks.py, policy/policy.pth, recommend.prototxt, train_test.prototxt) by design, because it is a search/planning optimizer, not a trained dcbrain RL policy; those responsibilities are relocated into planner/. One honest defect remains to remediate: AITrajectoryReplay calls run(policy="ai") without setting self.test_collector, so the template's run_ai() raises at runtime.

rows: distributed: planner/objective.py, kpi.py, monitor.py, oracle_worker.py, recommendation.py, broadcast.py, configs/dt/room2ite_map.json
src/data/ (EPW + schedule JSON kept; adds calibration/realized-history)
src/models/ (same building.json schema; idf/ subfolder; adds forecaster.pkl)

blocks.text: Legend: followed = contract honored as-is; adapted = honored with deliberate, justified divergences; partial = contract partly honored with a gap; missing = artifact absent (here, by architectural design); extended = template honored plus domain-specific additions.

DCTwin mirrors the dcwiz sample_template/ layout file-for-file where its architecture overlaps, and diverges only on the RL-training slots it does not need. Marks: match = same role/shape honored; + extension = honored plus a DCTwin-specific addition; absent = not present (every absence here is by design, the planner has no trained RL policy). All src/ rows below were verified against the repo.

blocks.caption: sample_template/ (dcwiz) vs src/ (DCTwin). Verified anchors: plan_weekly.py:11,22; ai_trajectory_test.py:14; baseline_policy_test.py:12; planner/beam_search.py.

blocks.text: DCTwin subclasses both dcwiz standard policy templates and mirrors the sample_template layout for configs/, data/, and models/. Each base-class contract (__init__ -> __call__ -> initialize -> run) is honored at the designated extension points. All DCTwin-side (src/...) citations below were re-verified against the repo. Template-side citations are taken as given from the source report (external dcwiz/dcbrain sample). Verdict vocabulary: followed = contract honored as-is; adapted = honored with justified divergences; partial = honored with a gap; missing = absent by design; extended = honored plus domain additions.

WeeklyPlanTemplate subclasses RecommendTemplate and imports it from dcwiz_policy_template, overriding initialize() to wire forecaster/oracle/beam-planner and fully replacing run() to call run_weekly_plan(). The base run() (which expects a reactive dcbrain policy and would call log_recommendations()) is never reached -- a justified divergence for a deterministic batch planner, not a contract violation.

rows: Base raises NotImplementedError; DCTwin sets forecaster, ParallelEnvOracle, BeamPlanner; self.policy stub at :63
Calls run_weekly_plan() at :73, write_recommendation() at :83-84; base policy call bypassed, log_recommendations() never reached

blocks.text: AITrajectoryReplay subclasses TrajectoryPolicyTemplate and overrides initialize() to set self.env and self.act (the fixed replay action expanded from the recommendation JSON), as the contract requires. The gap: __call__ is invoked with policy="ai" but self.test_collector is never set, so the template's run_ai() raises ValueError("Test collector is not set") at runtime. Fix: set self.test_collector in initialize(), or call with policy="baseline" (the fixed-action replay is baseline-shaped anyway). This is the one honest defect; everything else here is followed.

rows: self.env via dctwin.make_env at :20; self.act = mapper_from_env(self.env).expand(setpoints) at :28
GAP: policy="ai" with no self.test_collector -> run_ai() raises
__call__(policy="ai", ...) dispatches to run_ai(), which rejects an unset test_collector -- crashes as written

blocks.text: BaselineTrajectory honors the contract end-to-end: subclass, initialize() sets self.env and a conservative self.act (coolest SAT/CHW, maximum airflow), and the base run_baseline() runs unchanged via policy="baseline". No gaps; the only difference from the template is a benign encoding choice (parametrized Setpoints + broadcast vs hand-built per-zone arrays), with identical physical semantics.

rows: initialize() sets conservative self.act (coolest SAT/CHW, max airflow)
Setpoints(sat_c=s.sat.lb, flow_kg_s=s.flow.ub, chwst_c=s.chwst.lb) from DEFAULT_SEARCH_SPACE (types.py:81-85: sat[20,26], flow[4.8,13.8], chwst[13,19]); expanded at :20

title: Shared layouts honored: configs, data, models

blocks.text: The configs/, data/, and models/ trees mirror the dcwiz sample_template layout. Shared JSON schemas and protobuf shapes are honored exactly; DCTwin adds domain-specific files for its dual loop and omits only the RL-only configs. Every src/ fact below was verified against the repository.
configs/ — JSON schemas and prototxt shapes match

rows: Identical set of 13 top-level device keys (acus, chillers, ites, zones, cooling towers, ...); same schema, fewer device instances
zones = {air temperature, air relative humidity, ite power}; ites = {inlet dry-bulb temp, inlet RH, cpu/fan/ups power} — identical
Same protobuf sections (eplus_env_config; logging_config + policy_config + test_collector / off_policy_trainer)
Omitted by design (RL-only inference / combined train+test)

blocks.text: data/ — formats kept, dual-loop files added

rows: JSON-array schedules honored (acus nested under fan_on_off/); + pumps/ added for the CHW plant
Static policy sample replaced by per-15-min telemetry (adapted role)
Dual-loop additions: bias/sigma, paired predicted/realized weeks, weekly KPI history
Identical top-level keys: constructions, geometry, inputs, meta, models
Moved into an idf/ subfolder (logical grouping); same IDF format
Statistical forecaster config replaces policy.pth (2.9 MB neural weights) — model-prep slot

blocks.text: One architectural fact explains every "missing" verdict: DCTwin is a search/planning optimizer, not a trained RL agent. The dcwiz template assumes a dcbrain SAC policy persisted as policy/policy.pth, fed per-step rewards and states through hooks.py, and trained by ai_policy_train.py. DCTwin instead beam-searches three global setpoints (SAT, airflow, CHWST), scoring every candidate with a full-week EnergyPlus oracle run. That swap removes the RL training pipeline and relocates its responsibilities into the planner/ package and JSON config, verified present in src/ (all targets below confirmed).

blocks.caption: RL artifacts relocated into DCTwin's planner package and config.

blocks.text: One honest defect. AITrajectoryReplay (src/ai_trajectory_test.py:14) overrides initialize() at :17-28 to set self.env and self.act, but never sets self.test_collector, then is invoked with policy="ai" at :36. The template's run_ai() requires a test collector and raises ValueError("Test collector is not set"), so the call crashes as written. Fix, two options: set self.test_collector in initialize(), OR call with policy="baseline" (the fixed-action replay is baseline-shaped anyway). BaselineTrajectory (src/baseline_policy_test.py:12) is unaffected and fully followed.

items: RecommendTemplate: followed via adaptation. WeeklyPlanTemplate (plan_weekly.py:22) imports from dcwiz_policy_template (:11), overrides initialize() (:30) and run() (:67) at the designated extension points, and honors the __init__/__call__ lifecycle.

TrajectoryPolicyTemplate: followed for baseline, partial for AI replay. BaselineTrajectory honors the contract end-to-end via policy="baseline"; AITrajectoryReplay honors structure + initialize() but has the test_collector gap above.

configs/, data/, models/: honored with extensions. JSON device-map schemas, EPW weather, schedule-folder convention, and dt/test/train prototxt shapes all match; DCTwin adds calibration/realized-history, pumps/, and forecaster.pkl without breaking shared schemas.

RL-training artifacts (ai_policy_train.py, single-file hooks.py, policy/policy.pth, recommend.prototxt, train_test.prototxt): intentionally absent, replaced by fit_forecaster.py, the planner/ package, and BeamPlanner.

Net: the template contracts (base-class subclassing, the initialize/run/__call__ lifecycle, the config/data/model layouts) are honored. One gap to fix: the AITrajectoryReplay test_collector omission; everything else is a justified architectural adaptation.
